title: The Wolf in Sheep's Clothing
content:
Deep in the hills, there lived a hungry wolf. He had watched a flock of fat sheep grazing in the meadow for many days. But the shepherd was always watchful, and the wolf could never get close enough to catch a single sheep.

One morning, the wolf found a large sheepskin lying at the edge of the forest. He smiled slowly. He pulled the sheepskin over his back and trotted toward the flock.

To anyone glancing quickly, the wolf looked just like a sheep! His gray paws were hidden. His sharp teeth were tucked behind the soft, curly disguise.

He crept into the flock and the sheep paid him no attention. They thought he was simply another sheep joining them. The wolf walked happily among them, waiting for the perfect moment to strike.

All day the wolf walked with the flock. The sheep grazed peacefully, never guessing that danger walked among them. As the sun set, the shepherd called the animals into the pen for the night.

The shepherd reached into the pen to choose one sheep for supper. His hand closed around the wolf's woolly disguise. He pulled the sheepskin right off, revealing the wolf underneath!

The shepherd chased the wolf away with his staff and kept his flock safe. He shook his head and said, "A fine coat can hide an ugly heart. Things are not always what they appear to be."

author: Aesop
